brand.voice: Warm, plain-spoken, practical. An NDIS-world insider who built a tool to fix a real pain. Never salesy, never corporate. Australian spelling.
brand.founderStory: I work in disability support and got sick of the admin eating the hours that should go to participants. So I built a tool to fix it. Sharing what I learn along the way.
brand.disclosure: I built this app — when I mention it I'll always say so. Posts are from me, not a bot.

audience.primary: NDIS sole traders (independent support workers / allied health) in Australia
audience.secondary: Small and medium NDIS providers (2-50 staff)
pains: Admin and paperwork eating billable/support hours
Confusing NDIS price guide and line-item changes
Invoicing, claims and getting paid on time
audience.tone_notes: This audience is wary of being sold to and protective of participants. Lead with usefulness. Respect, never hype.

app.name: Caira
app.tagline: Care is the work. Caira handles the record.
app.oneLiner: Caira takes the documentation off a support worker's plate — notes, records and reporting handled, so the time goes back to care.
app.status: pre-launch — site not live yet; legal/go-to-market in progress
app.earlyInterest: Register interest / join the early list (replace with real waitlist URL or 'DM me' when ready)
app.mentionPolicy: Soft. App appears as 'the tool I built' only after value is established. Max ~1 in 4 posts references it in weeks 1-2.

arc.week1: Pure value + introduce yourself. Zero app pitch. Build trust and show you live in this world.
arc.week2: More value, first soft founder-story posts, one gentle 'here's what I'm building' beta invite.
arc.ongoing: 70% useful content, 20% community/conversation, 10% honest app updates + case studies.
